none: Havasupai Indian Reservation addition : environmental impact statement.

This document details the "Environmental Inventory Map Series" for the HavenSupai Indian Reservation in Arizona, prepared by the Applied Remote Sensing Program Office of arid lands studies. The map series, encompassing 10 designated areas, aims to assist in planning and development. It includes topographical maps, drainage routes, vegetation, and landforms, highlighting the terrain and potential for future development. The project focuses on identifying and mapping key areas, including canyons, streams, and grasslands. The HavenSupai Tribe is collaborating with the University of Arizona to create this comprehensive resource, aiming to facilitate sustainable land use and resource management within the reservation area. The map series is designed to aid in strategic planning and resource allocation for the region.
Havasupai Indian Reservation addition : environmental impact statement.

This document details the "Environmental Inventory Map Series" for the HavenSupai Indian Reservation in Arizona, prepared by the Applied Remote Sensing Program Office of arid lands studies. The map series, encompassing 10 designated areas, aims to assist in planning and development. It includes topographical maps, drainage routes, vegetation, and landforms, highlighting the terrain and potential for future development. The project focuses on identifying and mapping key areas, including canyons, streams, and grasslands. The HavenSupai Tribe is collaborating with the University of Arizona to create this comprehensive resource, aiming to facilitate sustainable land use and resource management within the reservation area. The map series is designed to aid in strategic planning and resource allocation for the region.
W. Fairmount Avenue; Joe P. Sparks; Carl; Jeanne Hullne; John Weiner

The project, initiated in 2005, aimed to create a comprehensive digital map of the Havasupai Reservation, leveraging remote sensing technology to create a detailed, easily navigable map of the landforms, drainage patterns, vegetation, and other features of the reservation. The goal was to facilitate efficient land management, resource allocation, and conservation efforts. However, due to funding constraints and shifting priorities, the project was ultimately scaled back to a smaller, more focused mapping effort, with limited implementation of the full digital map. Therefore, the project was completed, but not fully executed.
